title: Running the blobtest function
description:
Running the blobtest function

Running the main Function

You should be familiar with the Run window for Epson RC+ 8.0.  We will use the Run window to run our sample program that was created in the previous section.  The steps to accomplish this are shown below:

(1)    Click the [Open Run Window]button on the Epson RC+ 8.0 toolbar.  This will cause the Run window to appear.  Notice that in the figure below, the Run window is split into 2 parts.  The left half of the Run window is the image display (note the washer shown in the center) and the right half of the Run window is the text area which is used for displaying text messages.

If the Run window only shows the Text Area and doesn't appear with both an image display and a text area, click the Display Video check box on the Run window.

Run window with Image Display and Text Area

(2)    Click the [Start] button located at the bottom left corner of the Run window.  This will cause the function called "main" to run.

(3)    You can see sample results in the figure in Step (4) from running the function "main".  Notice that when the blob is found, it is highlighted in green in the image display on the left side of the Run window.  The right side of the Run window displays text that states that the blob was found and the area of the blob.

(4)    Double click the Control-menu box of the Run window that is located in the upper left corner of the Run window.  This will close the Run window.

A sample Run window display after running "main"

You should be familiar with the Run window for EPSON RC+.  We will use the Run window to run our sample program that was created in the previous section.  The steps to accomplish this are shown below:

	Click on the Run toolbar button  on
	 the EPSON RC+ toolbar.  This will cause the EPSON RC+ Run window
	 to appear.  Notice that in the figure below, the Run window is
	 split into 2 parts.  The left half of the Run window is the image
	 display area (note the washer shown in the center) and the right half
	 of the Run window is the text area which is used for displaying text
	 messages.

Note: If the Run window only shows the Text Area and doesn't appear with both an Image Display Area and a Text Area, click on the Display Video check box on the Run window.

Run window with Image and Text Display Area

	Click on the Start
	 button located at the bottom left corner of the Run window.  This
	 will cause the function called "main" to run.
	You can see sample results in the figure
	 below from running the function "main".  Notice that
	 when the blob is found, it is highlighted in green in the image display
	 area on the left side of the Run window.  The right side of the
	 Run window displays text that states that the blob was found and the
	 area of the blob.
	Double click the Control-menu box of the
	 Run window that is located in the upper left corner of the Run window.
	  This will close the Run window.
